data.title: Dow Jones futures drop after Presidents Day. S&P 500, Nasdaq below 50-day lines. Apple, Google, Amazon, Meta, Nvidia in focus.
data.description: Following Presidents Day closure of U.S. markets, futures showed a mixed trend. Although the S&P 500 and Nasdaq indexes are currently below their 50-day moving averages, tech giants such as Apple, Google, Amazon, Meta, and Nvidia are overshadowing some hidden market strength. Dow Jones futures are indicating a decline, with focus on the mentioned tech stalwarts.

data.title: Harvard University decreases Bitcoin holdings by 21% to 5.35 million shares of iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF, worth $265.80 million.新闻文章的总结：哈佛大学在第四季度削减了比特币持有量，并首次进入以太坊ETF。哈佛管理公司报告显示，到12月31日为止，其持有 iShares比特币信托ETF 的5.35百万股，较上一季度减少了21%。其中价值265.8百万美元，占总资产的12.78%。
data.description: According to its latest financial filing, Harvard University has reduced its Bitcoin position and entered the Ethereum market for the first time. Harvard Management Company, a subsidiary managing the university's finances, held 5.35 million shares of iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF as of Dec. 31, marking a 21% decrease from the previous quarter. The value of the stake was approximately $265.80 million based on IBIT's price of 49.65 on Dec. 31. Even with a smaller exposure, the Bitcoin holdings remained the largest in Harvard's portfolio, accounting for 12.78% of the total.

data.title: Therma-Tru, Larson, Fiberon, Fypon, and Solar Innovations showcase innovative designs at 2026 International Builders' Show.
data.description: A captivating multi-sensory installation is a highlight at the International Builders' Show, featuring a collaboration between Therma-Tru, Larson, Fiberon, Fypon, and Solar Innovations. The event in Deerfield, Illinois showcases the innovative partnership, aiming to engage visitors with a unique experience. Attendees can expect an immersive display that combines the expertise and products of these industry leaders. This installation underscores the commitment to innovation and creativity within the construction and building materials sector. Visitors have the opportunity to witness firsthand the combined efforts of these companies, offering a glimpse into the future of construction technology.

data.title: Logan Paul sells rare Pokémon card for $16.5M after NFT controversy, with investors suing over lost returns from Liquid Marketplace.
data.description: In 2022, Paul split ownership of a valuable Pokémon card on Liquid Marketplace before the site shut down. This decision triggered a legal battle as investors sought refunds for their shares.
